user_query:
I need a full, compliant overhaul of our Amazon review request process based on last-month performance. Please read input/product_data.csv and input/buyer_segments.json, then generate the following deliverables under output/:

1) Velocity report
- Write output/velocity_report.csv with columns exactly: ASIN, product_name, category, monthly_orders, last_30d_reviews, expected_orders_per_review, actual_orders_per_review, status
- Benchmarks to use for expected_orders_per_review by category: Electronics=50, Kitchenware=30, Apparel=40, Beauty/Personal Care=25, Toys/Games=35, Sports/Outdoors=40, Books=100
- Compute actual_orders_per_review = monthly_orders / last_30d_reviews (if last_30d_reviews=0, write "Infinity")
- Set status = "underperforming" if actual_orders_per_review > expected_orders_per_review OR last_30d_reviews=0; otherwise set status = "on_benchmark_or_better"

2) Priority queue
- Write output/priority_queue.csv with columns exactly: ASIN, review_gap, rank, prioritize_repeat_buyers
- Compute expected_reviews_at_benchmark = monthly_orders / expected_orders_per_review; review_gap = expected_reviews_at_benchmark - last_30d_reviews (round to 2 decimals)
- Include only rows where review_gap > 0 and sort by review_gap descending; rank starting at 1
- Set prioritize_repeat_buyers = "yes" for every row where buyer_segments indicate any repeat buyer share for the ASIN; otherwise "no"

3) Timing recommendations
- Write output/timing_recommendations.csv with columns exactly: ASIN, category, avg_delivery_days, recommended_start_day, recommended_end_day, rationale
- Use these day windows by category (inclusive): Electronics 7-10; Apparel 4-6; Kitchenware 4-7; Beauty/Personal Care 7-10; Toys/Games 4-7; Sports/Outdoors 4-7; Books 4-7
- Ensure recommended_start_day < recommended_end_day and both are integers within the category’s window; briefly justify in rationale (e.g., "post-setup window" or "first-use period")

4) Compliance report for existing messages
- From the existing_message field in input/product_data.csv, write output/compliance_report.csv with columns exactly: ASIN, status, issues
- Status must be "fail" if any prohibited phrasing is present, else "pass"; issues should be a semicolon-separated list of the exact phrases detected when status is "fail"
- Prohibited phrases/patterns to check against (case-insensitive substring match): "5-star", "5 star", "positive review only", "if you're happy", "discount", "% off", "refund", "rebate", "gift card", "free", "incentive", "promotion", "update your review", "change your review", "remove your review", "coupon"

5) Risk flags
- Write output/risk_flags.csv with columns exactly: ASIN, risk, reason
- Add a row with risk = "suppression_suspected" for any ASIN with monthly_orders >= 100 AND last_30d_reviews = 0; reason should be "0 reviews in last 30 days after >=100 orders"

6) Compliant templates for underperformers
- For each ASIN marked "underperforming" in output/velocity_report.csv, create two files: output/templates/<ASIN>_template1.md and output/templates/<ASIN>_template2.md
- Each template must: (a) be <= 150 words, (b) include the product_name, (c) follow a greeting → value acknowledgment → single honest ask → no-pressure close structure, (d) include the phrase "honest feedback" or "honest review", and (e) contain none of the prohibited phrases/patterns listed above
- Do not ask for more than one review; include no promotional content of any kind

7) Policy notes
- Write output/policy_notes.txt summarizing the key compliance constraints in 5-8 bullet points; it must include the exact phrase "one request per order" and explicitly mention no incentives, no positive-only language, and not messaging opt-outs/returns

Use only the provided inputs. Save all artifacts exactly to the paths above. If any assumptions are needed, state them briefly in the rationale field(s) and keep all messaging ToS-compliant.
